DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-24-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - DANIEL WONG, TEL. NO. 899-0334, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. MADELYN G. JORDAN-DAVIS OF FOSTER EMPLOYMENT LAW, TEL. NO. (510)763-1900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LEVI STRAUSS & CO.'s DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH AND FIFTH COUNTS OF THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has had three opportunities to sufficiently state a cause of action for breach of contract and for unpaid wages, and has failed to do so each time. The Second Amended Complaint fails to sufficiently plead the terms of the allegedly breached contract and Plaintiff's entitlement to recovery under the Labor Code. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-18-2014 Continued To Nov-05-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Peter H. Goldsmith, Esq. of Foster Employment Law, Tel. 510.763.1900, for defendant and moving party;
Daniel Wong, Tel. 415.699.0634, plaintiff and opposing party, appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: The matter was discussed. The Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT LEVI STRAUSS & CO.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff is proceeding in pro per and cannot recover attorney's fees. See PLCM Group, Inc. v. Drexler (2000) 22 Cal.4th 1084, 1091-1092. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Peter H. Goldsmith, Esq. of Foster Employment Law, Tel. 510.763.1900, for defendant and moving party;
Daniel Wong, Tel. 415.699.0634, plaintiff and opposing party, appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: The matter was argued. At end of arugment, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT LEVI STRAUSS & CO.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as follows: The demurrer to the age discrimination cause of action is overruled. Paragraph 16 of the amended complaint alleges that plaintiff's employment was terminated because of his age. The demurrer to the retaliation (CFRA) cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff alleges that he was retaliated against for providing information regarding family care per Cal. Gov't Code sec. 12945.2(l). Defendant's papers do not address that theory of liability. The demurrer to the wrongful termination cause of action is overruled because the FEHA and CFRA public policies can support the claim. The demurrers to causes of action 3, 4, and 5 (wage and contract claims) are sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead the terms of the contract and entitlement to recovery with more detail. The demurrer to the negligent misrepresentation cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not plead reasonable reliance. The demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff's allegations regarding age discrimination and retaliation support the claim and those allegations are not barred by worker's compensation exclusivity. See Fretland v. County of Humboldt, (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 1478, 1492. The demurrer to the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action is sustained without leave to amend because the complaint alleges intentional misconduct. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
